title: Clinopodium troodi
taxonomy: Clinopodium troodi is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It belongs to the genus Clinopodium, which is a group of plants commonly known as wild basil or gnat's bite. Clinopodium troodi is native to Cyprus and is often found in areas with limestone soils. The species is known to produce small, white or pinkish flowers that are highly fragrant and attractive to pollinators. Clinopodium troodi is a rare and endemic species, found only in the wild.

intro: Clinopodium troodi, also known as Troödi Clinopodium, is a perennial plant species native to Cyprus and Greece, belonging to the Lamiaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, providing relief from digestive issues, colds, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Clinopodium troodi include essential oils, flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, Clinopodium troodi is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in the production of essential oils and traditional herbal remedies.
